name: Mahide desde Figueruela de Arriba — circular por el Campo de Aliste
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Figueruela De Arriba are characterized by the varied terrain of the Sierra de la Culebra, featuring soft, rounded hills and significant elevation changes. The region boasts rich forestland and river valleys, irrigated by the Cabrón and Manzanas rivers, providing scenic routes through dense woodlands. Mountain bikers can expect a mix of less technical paths and more challenging unpaved segments, offering diverse experiences within this natural landscape. Best mountain bike trails around Figueruela De Arriba The most popular mountain bike trail is Linarejos and Rihonor de Castilla from Robledo — circular route through the Sierra de la Culebra, a 28.3 miles (45.6 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 10 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de la Culebra. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Caduerna MTB Trail, a moderate 23.8 miles (38.2 km) path. This trail offers engaging riding through varied terrain with notable elevation gains. Local mountain bikers also love the Puebla de Sanabria desde Robledo — circular por al Embalse de Cernadilla, a 31.8 miles (51.1 km) trail leading through the area around the Cernadilla reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. Mountain biking around Figueruela De Arriba is defined by the rolling hills of the Sierra de la Culebra, rich forest cover, and picturesque river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Figueruela De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from a few reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Figueruela De Arriba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Figueruela De Arriba?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Figueruela De Arriba, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 100 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: The terrain around Figueruela De Arriba, particularly in the Sierra de la Culebra, features soft, rounded hills with significant elevation changes. You'll find a mix of less technical, paved surfaces and more challenging unpaved segments, often winding through rich forestland and river valleys.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: Yes, the region offers options for various ability levels. While many trails are moderate, there are at least 2 easy routes available. These easier paths often feature less technical terrain and shorter distances, making them suitable for those new to mountain biking or families.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult mountain bike routes around Figueruela De Arriba. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1260409335/hide-from-linarejos-circular-on-the-mill-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hide from Linarejos — circular on the Mill Route&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 64 km with more than 1100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What are some scenic points of interest I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many routes offer panoramic views of the Sanabria region and the Sierra de la Culebra. You might also encounter natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5814010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Weir and Boardwalk of the Rio Onor&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fidionda Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The area's rich forest and river valleys provide picturesque settings throughout your ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1260473611/mahide-desde-figueruela-de-arriba-circular-por-el-campo-de-aliste" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mahide desde Figueruela de Arriba — circular por el Campo de Aliste&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 32.7 km loop.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: The mountain bike trails in Figueruela De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the Sierra de la Culebra, and the engaging experiences offered by routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018775114/la-caduerna-mtb-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caduerna MTB Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I observe wildlife while mountain biking in this region?
A: Yes, the Sierra de la Culebra is known for its rich fauna. You have the opportunity to observe wild animals, including deer and various birds. The Iberian wolf, the emblem of the Culebra mountain range, also inhabits the area, though sightings are rare.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: The region's diverse landscapes and varied tree cover make it enjoyable across different seasons. While specific seasonal conditions are not detailed, the presence of dense woodlands suggests pleasant rides during warmer months with ample shade, and potentially beautiful scenery in autumn.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by mountain bike?
A: The region has a rich history, including ancient Roman roads like the Vía XVII. While specific routes may not directly follow these, the historical context suggests old pathways that could be part of biking routes. You might also pass through historical settlements or near structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7173693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Stone Church&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific villages or settlements worth exploring near the trails?
A: Yes, the area includes charming settlements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1679368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio de Onor Village&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight. Exploring these villages can add a cultural dimension to your mountain biking adventure.
tips: The Campo de Aliste is a protected natural space in the province of Zamora located in a valley between the channels of the Aliste and Cebal rivers. Around here you can enjoy extensive pine and oak groves on the slopes and high areas and cereal fields in the low areas.
Gravel path through the Carbón stream, until reaching the rock formation called Antigua Losera de Figueruela. The path runs along the slopes of Peña Mira.
Mahide is a small hamlet in the Aliste region, in the province of Zamora, surrounded by a natural environment of great value such as the Sierra de la Culebra.

waypoints.name: Carbón Stream and Antigua Losera de Figueruela
